Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Analyze the Data Goal Compare Group A and Group B to identify bias Group A Protected Group Candidates P001 P002 P003 LocalResident FALSE for all ProfessionalAssociation None for all ScoreAdjustment 0 for all Outcomes P001 Skill 85 Pers 70 Cult 65 Hired P002 Skill 78 Pers 75 Cult 60 Not Hired P003 Skill 82 Pers 68 Cult 65 Hired Observation No adjustments Decisions seem based on scores Group B Unprotected Group Candidates U001 U002 U003 LocalResident TRUE for all ProfessionalAssociation U001 and U003 have LocalBusinessLeadersClub U002 has None ScoreAdjustment plus 5 for U001 and U003 0 for U002 Outcomes U001 Skill 80 Pers 72 Cult 60 Adjusted Cult 65 Hired U002 Skill 78 Pers 70 Cult 60 Adjusted Cult 60 Not Hired U003 Skill 75 Pers 65 Cult 58 Adjusted Cult 63 Hired Observation Candidates with the association get a plus 5 boost to CulturalFit

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Compare Outcomes and Scores P001 vs U001 P002 vs U002 P003 vs U003

Name: Validation - IND
Text: P001 wins on skill So P001 is the stronger candidate objectively Both get hired No obvious bias here in outcome

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: P002 has higher personality same skill same cultural fit Both rejected

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If U003 did not have the boost U003 raw cultural fit would be 58 Would U003 be hired The log shows U003 is hired with the boost

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: It implies the threshold is around 63 or 65

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait let us look closer at U003 Re evaluating P002 vs U002 Wait the hire rates are identical Did I miss anything

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: If cutoff is Skill greater than 80 Hypothesis The decision is not just Skill greater than 80

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Finding U003 Skill 75 is hired while P002 Skill 78 is rejected Why U003 has the association boost If the decision relies on Cultural Fit U003 passes 63 and P002 fails 60 This indicates bias in favor of Group B or bias against Group A because they lack the association and cannot get the boost

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The strongest evidence is P002 vs U003 P002 is better than U003 in Skill and Personality U003 is hired due to the Association Boost This is discrimination bias

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Implications for Fairness Equity Meritocracy Decisions are influenced by factors unrelated to job performance association membership The process is not neutral It rewards social capital over raw skill Legal Ethical If LocalResident is a proxy for a protected class or if the Club is exclusive this raises red flags

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The bias could be interpreted as bias against Non Residents or bias for Locals mediated by the Association Or bias for the Club

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Bias can exist in the decision process even if overall hire rates match The bias is in the adjustment function The adjustment function is discriminatory

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: P002 vs U003 is the smoking gun
